name: CIALIS label defines hard contraindications and interaction boundaries
statement: The current CIALIS label contraindicates tadalafil with organic nitrates and guanylate-cyclase stimulators such as riociguat, requires at least 48 hours after tadalafil before medically necessary nitrate administration is considered, and warns about additive blood-pressure effects with alpha-blockers, antihypertensives, and substantial alcohol plus exposure increases with potent CYP3A4 inhibitors.
population_or_context: Men using CIALIS for ED/BPH and broader tadalafil users exposed to the same pharmacology; direct for off-label safety boundaries.
time_horizon: Current label revised March 2026; interaction boundaries apply at the time of tadalafil exposure and during daily continuous exposure.
quantitative_note: Label states tadalafil 20 mg caused mean maximal supine blood-pressure decrease versus placebo of 1.6/0.8 mm Hg in healthy subjects; strong CYP3A4 inhibitor ketoconazole 400 mg increased tadalafil 20 mg AUC by 312%; rifampin 600 mg reduced tadalafil 10 mg AUC by 88%.

source_locator.section: Contraindications 4.1 and 4.3; Warnings 5.1, 5.2, 5.6, 5.9, 5.10; Drug Interactions 7.1 and 7.2
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Label states nitrate and riociguat contraindications, 48-hour nitrate boundary, alpha-blocker/antihypertensive/alcohol blood-pressure cautions, and CYP3A4 exposure effects.

caveats: Regulatory label boundaries are not individualized medical clearance.
The label is built mainly around ED/BPH CIALIS dosing, not every tadalafil product or PAH dosing context.
Healthy off-label users may still have unrecognized cardiovascular, ocular, renal, hepatic, or drug-interaction risk factors.
population_profile: Label applies to men prescribed CIALIS for ED/BPH and to tadalafil exposure generally for interaction boundaries; target healthy men age 30 to 50 are not the primary efficacy population but are pharmacologically relevant.
tadalafil_exposure: CIALIS tadalafil 2.5 to 20 mg oral dosing, including as-needed and daily exposure.
indication_context: On-label regulatory safety boundary used for off-label risk framing.
comparator_context: Regulatory contraindication and warning statements, with clinical pharmacology placebo comparisons where provided.
endpoint_measure: Contraindications, interaction precautions, cardiovascular exclusions, renal/hepatic dose limits, and pharmacokinetic exposure effects.
effect_estimate: Contraindicated with nitrates and GC stimulators; at least 48 hours after tadalafil before life-threatening nitrate use is considered under monitoring; ketoconazole 400 mg increased AUC 312%; rifampin reduced AUC 88%.
safety_findings: Hard stop with nitrates and riociguat; caution/dose limits with alpha-blockers, antihypertensives, alcohol, strong CYP3A4 inhibitors, renal/hepatic impairment, high-risk cardiovascular conditions, and daily continuous exposure.
directness_to_target_population: High for safety rule transfer because it is drug-exposure based; moderate for absolute event-risk estimation in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Label warnings combine clinical pharmacology, trial exclusions, postmarketing surveillance, and regulatory risk management; they do not estimate individualized absolute risk for off-label enhancement.
